description: PM-KISAN is a Central Sector scheme that provides income support of Rs. 6,000 per year in three equal installments of Rs. 2,000 each to all land-holding farmer families across the country. The income support is directly transferred to the bank accounts of the farmers.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Rs. 6,000 per year (Rs. 2,000 per installment, 3 times a year) direct bank transfer to farmer's account.
apply_steps: Visit PM-KISAN portal pmkisan.gov.in or nearest Common Service Centre (CSC)
Click on 'New Farmer Registration' and enter Aadhaar number
Fill in personal and land details
Submit and verify via OTP on Aadhaar-linked mobile
Wait for approval from local Patwari/Revenue officer who verifies land records

description: PMAY-Gramin aims to provide pucca houses with basic amenities to all houseless and those living in dilapidated and kutcha houses in rural areas. The central government provides financial assistance for construction of houses to eligible BPL families in rural areas.
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 1,20,000 (plain areas) or Rs. 1,30,000 (hilly/difficult areas) for house construction. Additional Rs. 12,000 for toilet construction under SBM.
documents: Bank account passbook (preferably joint account with female member)
Land ownership documents or consent from Gram Panchayat
apply_steps: Check if your name is in the SECC 2011 permanent waitlist on awaassoft.nic.in
If eligible, local Gram Panchayat or Block Development Officer (BDO) will initiate the process
Submit application with Aadhaar and bank details at Gram Panchayat
House construction is monitored via AwaasSoft portal with geo-tagged photos
Payment released in installments: 1st after foundation, 2nd at lintel, 3rd on completion

scheme_name: MGNREGS (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme)
description: MGNREGS guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work. It provides livelihood security to rural families and creates durable community assets.
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
benefits: 100 days of guaranteed wage employment per year per household. Wages paid within 15 days of work. Unemployment allowance if work not provided within 15 days of demand.
benefit_amount: State-notified minimum wage (₹220–₹350/day depending on state)
apply_steps: Apply for Job Card at the local Gram Panchayat — free of cost
Gram Panchayat issues Job Card within 15 days
Submit written application to Gram Panchayat demanding work (at least 5 persons together)
Gram Panchayat provides work within 15 days or pays unemployment allowance
Wages credited to bank/post office account within 15 days of work completion

description: PMJDY is a national financial inclusion mission to ensure access to financial services for all households — banking, credit, insurance, and pension. It provides a zero-balance savings bank account with a RuPay debit card and accidental insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh.
ministry: Ministry of Finance
benefits: Zero-balance savings account, RuPay debit card, accidental insurance Rs. 2 lakh, life cover Rs. 30,000, overdraft facility up to Rs. 10,000 after 6 months of satisfactory operation, access to pension and insurance products.
documents: Voter ID / PAN card / Passport / NREGA Job Card / Driving Licence
apply_steps: Visit any bank branch or Bank Mitra (Business Correspondent) near you
Fill the account opening form (Form A) — available at branch or download from bank website
Submit identity and address proof
Account opened on same day — zero balance required
RuPay card issued within 10–15 working days

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY (Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana)
description: PM-JAY is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme. It provides health cover of Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to the bottom 40% of India's population (approx. 12 crore families) identified through SECC data.
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefits: Health coverage of Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for hospitalization. Covers pre and post hospitalization expenses, medicines, diagnostics, ICU charges. Cashless and paperless treatment at 25,000+ empanelled hospitals.
documents: Ayushman card (issued free — can be obtained at empanelled hospital or CSC)
apply_steps: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in or call 14555 (toll-free)
Visit nearest Ayushman Mitra at empanelled hospital or Common Service Centre
Get Ayushman Bharat card issued free using Aadhaar
For treatment, visit any empanelled government or private hospital
Show Ayushman card — treatment is cashless and paperless

description: PMFBY provides comprehensive crop insurance to farmers at very low premiums to protect against crop loss or damage due to unforeseen events — natural calamities, pests, and diseases. It covers food crops, oilseeds, and commercial/horticultural crops.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Crop insurance covering loss from drought, flood, cyclone, hailstorm, pest attack. Premium as low as 1.5% for Rabi crops, 2% for Kharif crops, 5% for horticulture. Full sum insured paid on crop loss.
benefit_amount: Premium: 1.5%–5% of sum insured (rest borne by government)
apply_steps: Loanee farmers are automatically enrolled by banks at the time of crop loan
Non-loanee farmers: visit nearest bank branch, CSC, or insurance company office before cut-off date
Fill PMFBY application form with crop and land details
Pay premium amount (rest subsidized by central and state governments)
In case of crop loss, inform bank/insurance company/government within 72 hours of damage

description: Kisan Credit Card scheme provides farmers with timely and adequate credit for their agricultural needs including cultivation, post-harvest expenses, maintenance of farm assets, and allied activities. KCC also covers personal accident insurance and asset insurance.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Revolving credit up to Rs. 3 lakh at 7% interest (4% effective after interest subvention). Covers crop loans, post-harvest expenses, allied activities. Personal accident cover Rs. 2 lakh (death) and Rs. 1 lakh (disability).
benefit_amount: Credit up to ₹3,00,000 at 4% effective interest
apply_steps: Visit any cooperative bank, regional rural bank, or commercial bank
Fill KCC application form with personal, land, and crop details
Bank assesses credit limit based on land holding and crop pattern
KCC issued as a debit/credit card within 2 weeks
Withdraw credit as needed during crop cycle; repay after harvest

description: PM Ujjwala Yojana provides free LPG connections to women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) households to replace unclean cooking fuels like wood, coal, and dung cakes, reducing indoor air pollution and health risks especially for rural women and children.
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
benefits: Free LPG connection with first refill and stove. Deposit-free connection (deposit of Rs. 1,600–2,600 borne by government). Easy EMI for stove and first refill repayable through future subsidy.
documents: BPL ration card OR SECC 2011 inclusion OR self-declaration of BPL status (for Ujjwala 2.0)
apply_steps: Visit nearest LPG distributor of IOCL, BPCL, or HPCL
Fill Form KYC-1 — application for LPG connection under PMUY
Submit Aadhaar, bank passbook, and BPL/SECC proof
Distributor verifies documents and submits to oil company
Connection released within 7 days; first cylinder and stove delivered at home

scheme_name: NSFDC Term Loan Scheme for SC Entrepreneurs
description: NSFDC (National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation) provides concessional loans to Scheduled Caste individuals below double the poverty line for income-generating activities including agriculture, small business, artisan work, and education.
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
benefits: Concessional loans at 6% per annum (2% for women) up to Rs. 20 lakh for income-generating activities. Covers agriculture, animal husbandry, transport, small business, artisan activities.
documents: SC caste certificate issued by competent authority
Project report for the proposed activity
apply_steps: Contact State Channelizing Agency (SCA) in your state — each state has one, e.g. BSSCC in Bihar
Fill NSFDC loan application form
Submit with caste certificate, income proof, and project report
SCA reviews and forwards to NSFDC for approval
Loan disbursed through SCA to your bank account

scheme_name: NSTFDC Term Loan for ST Entrepreneurs
description: NSTFDC (National Scheduled Tribes Finance and Development Corporation) provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribes for economic development activities below double the poverty line. It covers agriculture, forest-based activities, small businesses, and education.
ministry: Ministry of Tribal Affairs
benefits: Concessional loans at 6% per annum (4% for women) up to Rs. 25 lakh. Covers agriculture, forest produce collection, tribal art, transport, and small business.
documents: ST caste certificate from competent authority
apply_steps: Visit State Channelizing Agency (STCA) in your state
Obtain and fill NSTFDC application form
Attach ST certificate and income certificate
STCA forwards application to NSTFDC after local verification
Loan sanctioned and disbursed within 60 days

scheme_name: PM Scholarship Scheme for Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) Wards
description: PM Scholarship Scheme provides scholarships to wards and widows of CAPF personnel including CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, NSG, AR for pursuing professional degree courses. The scheme supports education of children of martyrs and disabled personnel.
ministry: Ministry of Home Affairs
benefits: Rs. 2,500/month for boys and Rs. 3,000/month for girls for 4-5 year professional courses (Engineering, Medical, MBA, MCA, Pharmacy, Architecture, etc.).
documents: Service certificate / death certificate of parent (CAPF personnel)
Mark sheet of qualifying exam (minimum 60% marks)
apply_steps: Apply online at National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in)
Login/register and select 'PM Scholarship for CAPF' scheme
Fill details, upload documents — service certificate of parent is mandatory
Institution verifies and forwards
Scholarship credited to bank account in each semester

description: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana is a small savings scheme for the girl child in India. It encourages parents to save for future education and marriage expenses. Opened in name of girl child below 10 years, it offers the highest interest rate among government-backed small savings schemes.
ministry: Ministry of Finance
benefits: 8.2% per annum interest (tax-free under 80C). Minimum deposit Rs. 250/year. Matures in 21 years. Partial withdrawal allowed for girl's education after she turns 18. Full tax exemption on deposit, interest, and maturity.
apply_steps: Visit any post office or authorized bank branch (SBI, Bank of Baroda, Canara Bank, etc.)
Fill Form 1 — Application for opening Sukanya Samriddhi Account
Submit with birth certificate of girl child and parent's Aadhaar
Make initial deposit (Rs. 250 minimum, Rs. 1.5 lakh maximum per year)
Account passbook issued immediately

description: PMKVY is the flagship skill development scheme under Skill India Mission. It provides short-duration skill training aligned with National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF) to youth. Training is free and includes monetary reward on successful certification.
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
benefits: Free skill training (150–300 hours) in 30+ sectors (Electronics, Construction, Retail, Agriculture, Healthcare etc.). Monetary reward Rs. 500 to Rs. 7,500 on certification. Placement assistance after training.
documents: Educational qualification certificate (8th pass minimum preferred)
apply_steps: Visit skillindiadigital.gov.in or nearest PMKVY Training Centre
Register online with Aadhaar and choose a skill course
Attend training at empanelled Training Partner centre near you
Appear for assessment exam conducted by Sector Skill Council
Receive certificate and monetary reward directly to bank account

description: PMEGP provides credit-linked subsidy for setting up micro-enterprises in non-farm sectors. It aims at generating employment in rural and urban areas by helping unemployed youth and traditional artisans establish self-employment ventures.
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)
benefits: Government subsidy (margin money) of 15%–35% on project cost. For rural areas: 25% for General, 35% for SC/ST/OBC/Minority/Women/Ex-servicemen. Maximum project cost Rs. 50 lakh (manufacturing), Rs. 20 lakh (service).
benefit_amount: 15%–35% subsidy on project cost (up to ₹50 lakh)
apply_steps: Apply online at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal
Fill application with personal, educational, and project details
Submit to nearest KVIC (Khadi and Village Industries Commission) or KVIB or DIC office
Application reviewed and interview conducted
Loan sanctioned by bank, subsidy (margin money) deposited in bank account

description: JSY is a safe motherhood intervention under NHM (National Health Mission) that promotes institutional delivery among poor pregnant women to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality. Cash assistance is given directly to the woman to deliver at a government health facility.
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefits: Cash assistance of Rs. 1,400 (rural) and Rs. 1,000 (urban) in LPS states like Bihar, UP, Rajasthan, MP, Odisha, Jharkhand, Uttarakhand, J&K. Includes ASHA incentive of Rs. 600.
documents: MCH card / ANC registration at ASHA or Sub-centre
BPL card (in HPS states — not mandatory for LPS states like Bihar)
apply_steps: Register pregnancy with local ASHA (Accredited Social Health Activist)
Attend at least 3 antenatal checkups at Sub-centre, PHC, or CHC
Deliver at government or accredited private health facility
Submit MCH card, Aadhaar, and bank details to ASHA or ANM
Cash transferred to bank account within 7 days of delivery

description: MUDRA (Micro Units Development and Refinance Agency) provides loans up to Rs. 20 lakh to small/micro businesses in the non-farm sector without collateral. Three categories: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishore (Rs. 50,001–5 lakh), Tarun (Rs. 5–20 lakh).
ministry: Ministry of Finance
benefits: Collateral-free loans up to Rs. 20 lakh for non-farm micro/small businesses. Covers manufacturing, trading, and service enterprises. Competitive interest rates through banks, NBFCs, MFIs.
documents: Bank account statement (last 6 months if existing business)
apply_steps: Visit nearest bank branch (public/private), NBFC, or MFI
Fill MUDRA loan application form with business plan
For Shishu: minimal documentation, quick disbursal (usually 7–10 days)
Bank evaluates business plan and creditworthiness
Loan sanctioned and MUDRA card (working capital) issued

description: NAPS promotes apprenticeship training by sharing 25% of the stipend (up to Rs. 1,500/month) paid by employers to apprentices. It aims to link industry with trained youth to provide hands-on workplace experience with government support.
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
benefits: Stipend support: 25% of prescribed stipend or Rs. 1,500 per month (whichever is less) reimbursed by government to employer. Apprentice gets on-the-job training + monthly stipend from employer.
benefit_amount: Stipend (employer pays, govt reimburses 25% up to ₹1,500/month)
apply_steps: Register at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in as an apprentice seeker
Search for apprenticeship opportunities by trade, sector, and location
Apply directly to establishment offering apprenticeship
Sign apprenticeship agreement with employer
Report to employer — stipend paid monthly, government share reimbursed to employer

description: PMSBY is an accidental death and disability insurance scheme with very low premium. It provides coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death and total permanent disability at a premium of just Rs. 20 per year auto-debited from bank account.
ministry: Ministry of Finance
benefits: Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death or total permanent disability. Rs. 1 lakh for partial permanent disability. Premium: Rs. 20/year auto-debited from bank account.
apply_steps: Visit bank branch or use net banking/mobile banking
Fill PMSBY enrollment form or enable via SMS/net banking
Provide consent for Rs. 20 auto-debit per year from bank account
Policy activated immediately — certificate issued by bank
In case of accident: claim at bank with FIR, hospital/death certificate

description: PMJJBY is a life insurance scheme providing coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for death due to any reason (not limited to accidents) at a premium of Rs. 436 per year. It targets working-age population with bank accounts who lack life insurance.
ministry: Ministry of Finance
benefits: Life insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh for death by any cause. Premium: Rs. 436/year (auto-debited from bank account in May/June).
apply_steps: Enroll via bank branch, online banking, or mobile app of your bank
Fill PMJJBY enrollment form with nominee details
Provide consent for Rs. 436 annual auto-debit
Coverage starts immediately on premium debit
Claim: nominee submits death certificate + bank details at bank within 30 days

description: Atal Pension Yojana provides a guaranteed pension to workers in the unorganised sector. Subscribers get a fixed pension of Rs. 1,000–Rs. 5,000 per month from age 60. The government co-contributes 50% of the subscriber's contribution or Rs. 1,000/year (whichever is lower) for 5 years for eligible subscribers.
ministry: Ministry of Finance
benefits: Guaranteed pension of Rs. 1,000–5,000/month from age 60. Spouse continues pension after subscriber's death. Corpus returned to nominee after death of both. Government co-contribution for eligible members who joined before Dec 2015.
apply_steps: Open savings bank account if not already done
Visit bank branch or enroll via net/mobile banking
Fill APY Subscriber Registration Form
Select pension amount (Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000)
Monthly contribution auto-debited from bank account based on age and chosen pension

description: The Bihar Student Credit Card Scheme provides education loans of up to Rs. 4 lakh at 4% interest (1% for girls/transgender/disabled) to students of Bihar who have passed Class 12 and wish to pursue higher education in India. No collateral or guarantor required.
ministry: Education Department, Government of Bihar
benefits: Interest-free education loan up to Rs. 4 lakh at 4% (1% for girls/disabled/transgender). Covers tuition, hostel, books, laptop, food. Repayment starts only after getting employment.
apply_steps: Register at 7nishchay-yuvaupmission.bihar.gov.in
Fill online application with personal, educational, and institution details
Upload all documents
Visit District Registration and Counselling Centre (DRCC) in your district for verification
Loan approved and disbursed directly to institution and student account

description: Bihar government's scheme to provide financial assistance to BPL families for the marriage of daughters. It aims to discourage child marriage and encourage delayed marriage. A one-time grant is given at the time of daughter's marriage.
ministry: Social Welfare Department, Government of Bihar
benefits: Rs. 5,000 one-time grant to the girl's family at the time of marriage. Cheque issued in the name of the girl. Aims to prevent dowry and encourage legal marriage registration.
documents: Aadhaar card of girl and parents
Age proof of girl (18 years or above) — school certificate or birth certificate
Marriage invitation card or marriage certificate
apply_steps: Apply at block office (Prakhanda Karyalay) or Gram Panchayat
Fill Mukhyamantri Kanya Vivah application form
Attach BPL certificate, Aadhaar, age proof, and bank account details
Block Development Officer (BDO) verifies eligibility
Cheque issued in girl's name after verification

description: Bihar government scheme providing Rs. 10 lakh grant-cum-loan to SC/ST individuals to set up new enterprises. 50% is grant (non-repayable) and 50% is interest-free loan repayable over 7 years.
ministry: Industries Department, Government of Bihar
benefits: Rs. 10 lakh total assistance per enterprise: Rs. 5 lakh grant (non-repayable) + Rs. 5 lakh interest-free loan. Covers manufacturing, service, and trade enterprises.
documents: SC/ST caste certificate from Bihar authority
Project report for the proposed enterprise
apply_steps: Apply online at udyami.bihar.gov.in during application window
Fill personal details, caste certificate, and enterprise proposal
Upload all documents
Selection by lottery system if applications exceed available slots
Training provided; grant disbursed in installments after enterprise setup

scheme_name: Uttar Pradesh Kisan Karj Rahat Yojana
description: UP state scheme for waiving agricultural crop loans up to Rs. 1 lakh for small and marginal farmers in Uttar Pradesh. It provides direct relief to indebted farmers and helps restore their creditworthiness.
ministry: Agriculture Department, Government of Uttar Pradesh
benefits: Crop loan waiver up to Rs. 1 lakh per farmer from cooperative banks and scheduled commercial banks. Improves credit score for fresh loans.
documents: Loan passbook from cooperative or commercial bank
apply_steps: Visit the UP Kisan Karj Rahat portal: upkisankarjrahat.upsdc.gov.in
Enter loan account number and Aadhaar to check eligibility
If eligible, submit grievance or confirmation at portal
Bank verifies and applies waiver to loan account
Confirmation SMS sent to registered mobile number

description: PM SVANidhi provides working capital micro-loans to street vendors to restart livelihoods impacted by COVID-19. Vendors can get loans of Rs. 10,000 (1st), Rs. 20,000 (2nd), and Rs. 50,000 (3rd) with interest subsidy on timely repayment.
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
benefits: Collateral-free working capital loan: Rs. 10,000 (1st loan), Rs. 20,000 (2nd), Rs. 50,000 (3rd). 7% interest subsidy on timely repayment. Digital transaction incentive up to Rs. 1,200/year.
documents: Certificate of vending (CoV) or Letter of Recommendation (LoR) from Urban Local Body
apply_steps: Visit pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in or contact Urban Local Body (Nagar Panchayat/Municipality)
Obtain Certificate of Vending or Letter of Recommendation
Apply via SVANidhi portal, bank branch, or NBFC-MFI
Submit Aadhaar and vending certificate
Loan disbursed within 30 days to bank account

description: PMGSY aims to provide all-weather road connectivity to unconnected rural habitations. It funds construction and upgrade of rural roads and bridges. While it is a community/village-level scheme, it directly benefits rural residents by improving access to markets, hospitals, and schools.
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
benefits: All-weather road connectivity for villages. Habitations with 500+ population (250+ in hilly/tribal areas) get connected roads. Improves access to markets, health, and education.
documents: No individual application — community/Gram Panchayat resolution
Gram Sabha resolution submitted to District Panchayat
apply_steps: Gram Panchayat passes a resolution requesting road connectivity
Submitted to District Panchayat or District Rural Development Agency (DRDA)
DRDA selects habitations based on population and priority list
DPR (Detailed Project Report) prepared and submitted to state/central for approval
Road construction awarded to contractor and completed within 18 months

scheme_name: PM-KUSUM (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan)
description: PM-KUSUM promotes solar energy for farmers by subsidizing solar pumps and solarisation of grid-connected pumps. It helps farmers reduce electricity costs, ensures reliable irrigation, and earns additional income by selling surplus solar power to grid.
ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
benefits: Component A: 2 MW solar plants on farmer's land — farmer earns by selling power. Component B: 60% subsidy (30% central + 30% state) on standalone solar pumps up to 7.5 HP. Component C: Solarisation of grid-connected pumps.
apply_steps: Apply to State Nodal Agency (e.g., BREDA in Bihar, UPNEDA in UP)
Fill PM-KUSUM application form specifying component needed
Submit land and Aadhaar documents
10% farmer contribution paid; 60% subsidy applied upfront
Solar pump/plant installed by empanelled vendor

description: The National SC/ST Hub scheme supports SC/ST entrepreneurs to participate in public procurement. It provides skill upgradation, market linkage, handholding support, and preference in government procurement to SC/ST owned enterprises.
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
benefits: Preference in government procurement (4% sub-target for SC/ST enterprises). Free handholding support, skill training, market linkage, legal/financial advisory, credit facilitation.
apply_steps: Register enterprise at udyamregistration.gov.in
Register on GeM portal (gem.gov.in) as seller
Contact nearest National SC/ST Hub facilitation centre
Access free training, mentoring, and credit facilitation
Bid for government tenders with SC/ST procurement preference

description: PM Kisan Maan Dhan Yojana is a pension scheme for small and marginal farmers aged 18-40 years. It provides a minimum assured pension of Rs. 3,000 per month after the age of 60 years.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Minimum assured pension of Rs. 3,000 per month after age 60. Spouse gets 50% family pension on farmer's death. Government matches farmer's monthly contribution.
documents: Land records showing small or marginal farmer status
apply_steps: Visit nearest Common Service Centre
Register with Aadhaar and bank details
Choose monthly contribution based on age of entry
Contribution auto-debited from PM-KISAN installment or bank account
Pension starts at age 60 automatically

description: PKVY promotes organic farming in India through cluster-based approaches. It provides financial assistance to farmers to adopt organic farming practices and get their produce certified, helping them earn premium prices.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Rs. 50,000 per hectare over 3 years for organic inputs, certification, and marketing. Cluster-based approach covers minimum 20 hectares per cluster with 20 or more farmers.
apply_steps: Contact nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra or Agriculture Department
Form a cluster of minimum 20 farmers
Register cluster with district agriculture office
Receive organic inputs and training
Get PGS-India certification for premium market access

description: The Soil Health Card Scheme provides farmers with soil health cards that carry crop-wise recommendations of nutrients and fertilizers required for individual farms to help farmers improve productivity through judicious use of inputs.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Free soil testing and Soil Health Card with crop-wise fertilizer recommendations. Helps reduce input costs by avoiding excess fertilizer use. Card updated every 2 years.
apply_steps: Contact nearest Agriculture Department office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra
Request soil sample collection from your field
Soil testing done at government lab free of cost
Soil Health Card issued within 30 days
Follow crop-wise fertilizer recommendations on card

description: RKVY incentivizes states to increase public investment in agriculture and allied sectors. It funds innovative projects in crop development, horticulture, animal husbandry, fisheries, and agricultural infrastructure at the state level.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Grants for farm mechanization, irrigation, storage, horticulture, organic farming, and agri-business. Individual farmers benefit through state-implemented projects and subsidized equipment.
benefit_amount: Varies by state project — subsidy on equipment and infrastructure
apply_steps: Visit district Agriculture Department office
Enquire about current RKVY projects in your district
Apply for individual components like farm machinery subsidy
District office processes application
Subsidy credited or equipment provided directly

description: PMKSY aims to achieve convergence of investments in irrigation at field level, expand cultivable area under assured irrigation, improve on-farm water use efficiency, introduce sustainable water conservation practices, and ensure more crop per drop.
ministry: Ministry of Jal Shakti
benefits: 55% subsidy on drip and sprinkler irrigation systems for small and marginal farmers. 45% subsidy for other farmers. Covers equipment cost, installation, and training.
apply_steps: Apply at district Agriculture Department
Fill PMKSY application with land details
Select drip or sprinkler system as per crop and land
Vendor installs system after approval
Subsidy released directly to vendor; farmer pays balance

description: AIF provides medium to long-term debt financing for investment in agriculture infrastructure projects at farm gate and aggregation points. It covers cold storage, warehouses, processing units, sorting and grading units for farmers and FPOs.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Loans up to Rs. 2 crore with 3% interest subvention per annum for up to 7 years. Credit guarantee coverage under CGTMSE for loans up to Rs. 2 crore. Covers cold storage, warehouse, processing units.
benefit_amount: 3% interest subvention on loans up to ₹2 crore
apply_steps: Apply online at agriinfra.dac.gov.in
Submit project proposal with detailed business plan
Bank evaluates proposal and sanctions loan
3% interest subvention applied by government
Credit guarantee provided for eligible loans

description: NBHM promotes scientific beekeeping to increase honey production, improve crop pollination, and create additional income for farmers. It provides training, bee colonies, and equipment subsidies to beekeepers.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Subsidy on bee boxes, bee colonies, honey extractors, and processing equipment. Free training in modern beekeeping. Market linkage through NAFED and cooperatives.
benefit_amount: Subsidy on beekeeping equipment + free training
apply_steps: Contact nearest horticulture department or KVIC office
Register as beekeeper with district horticulture officer
Attend free beekeeping training program
Receive subsidized bee boxes and colonies
Link with NAFED or cooperative for honey marketing

description: PMMSY aims to bring about Blue Revolution through sustainable and responsible development of fisheries sector in India. It provides financial assistance to fishermen, fish farmers, and fish workers for infrastructure, modernization, and insurance.
ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
benefits: 40% subsidy for general category and 60% for SC/ST/Women on fisheries infrastructure. Covers fish ponds, cages, boats, equipment, cold storage, and fish feed. Fishermen insurance of Rs. 5 lakh.
apply_steps: Apply at district fisheries office or state fisheries department
Fill PMMSY application with project details
Submit with land/water body proof and bank details
District committee approves based on project viability
Subsidy released in installments as project progresses

description: AHIDF incentivizes private investment in dairy and meat processing infrastructure and animal feed plants. It helps individual entrepreneurs, FPOs, MSMEs, and private companies set up modern animal husbandry enterprises.
ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
benefits: 3% interest subvention on loans for dairy processing, meat processing, and animal feed plants. Credit guarantee for loans up to Rs. 2 crore. Covers dairy, poultry, and piggery infrastructure.
apply_steps: Apply online through scheduled commercial banks
Submit project proposal for dairy or meat processing unit
Bank sanctions loan with 3% government interest subvention
Construction and commissioning of unit
Regular monitoring by district animal husbandry office

description: DEDS promotes setting up of modern dairy farms and supporting infrastructure for dairy sector development. It provides back-ended capital subsidy through NABARD for purchase of milch animals, dairy equipment, and milk processing units.
ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
benefits: 25% back-ended capital subsidy for general category (33% for SC/ST) on project cost. Covers purchase of milch animals, purchase of heifer calves, vermicompost units, bulk milk coolers, dairy equipment.
apply_steps: Approach nearest commercial bank, cooperative bank, or regional rural bank
Submit DEDS project proposal with cost estimates
Bank sanctions loan and forwards to NABARD
NABARD releases back-ended subsidy to bank after project completion
Subsidy adjusted against loan outstanding

description: PMSMA provides fixed day assured and quality antenatal care services to pregnant women on the 9th of every month at government health facilities. It ensures every pregnant woman receives at least one checkup by a specialist doctor.
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefits: Free antenatal checkup by specialist or MBBS doctor on 9th of every month. Free blood tests, urine tests, ultrasound, and medicines. High-risk pregnancies identified and referred for safe delivery.
apply_steps: Register pregnancy with nearest ASHA worker or Sub-centre
Visit nearest PHC, CHC, or District Hospital on 9th of any month
Show MCH card for checkup
Receive free tests and specialist consultation
High-risk pregnancies referred to higher facility automatically

description: RBSK provides early identification and intervention for children from birth to 18 years for diseases, deficiencies, disabilities, and developmental delays. Mobile health teams screen children at schools and anganwadis free of cost.
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefits: Free screening for 30+ health conditions including heart disease, hearing loss, vision defects, club foot, cleft lip, sickle cell anemia. Free treatment and surgery at District Early Intervention Centres and tertiary hospitals.
benefit_amount: Free screening and treatment up to 18 years
documents: Aadhaar card of child or parent
apply_steps: Mobile health teams visit schools and anganwadis automatically
Children screened for 30+ conditions free of cost
Suspected conditions referred to Block Health Team
Confirmed cases referred to District Early Intervention Centre
Free treatment, surgery, and rehabilitation provided

description: NMHP aims to ensure availability and accessibility of minimum mental health care for all in the foreseeable future. It provides free mental health services through District Mental Health Programme clinics at government hospitals.
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefits: Free outpatient mental health consultation at district hospitals. Free medicines for mental disorders. Counseling and psychosocial support. Helpline iCall: 9152987821.
benefit_amount: Free mental health care and medicines
apply_steps: Visit nearest District Hospital or Community Health Centre
Ask for DMHP (District Mental Health Programme) OPD
Consultation with psychiatrist or psychologist free of cost
Free medicines provided from government pharmacy
Follow-up care at same facility

scheme_name: Nikshay Poshan Yojana (TB Patient Nutrition Support)
description: Nikshay Poshan Yojana provides nutritional support of Rs. 500 per month to tuberculosis patients during their treatment period to improve treatment outcomes and reduce mortality from TB.
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefits: Rs. 500 per month direct bank transfer for entire duration of TB treatment. Free TB medicines under RNTCP. Free diagnostic tests including X-ray and sputum examination.
documents: Aadhaar card linked to bank account
TB diagnosis certificate from government DOTS centre
apply_steps: Get diagnosed at nearest government DOTS centre or hospital
Register on Nikshay portal by health worker
Aadhaar and bank details submitted by DOTS provider
Rs. 500 credited to bank account every month automatically
Continue treatment for full duration as prescribed

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students
description: Post Matric Scholarship provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students studying at post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education. It covers maintenance allowance, study tour charges, and other essential expenses.
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
benefits: Maintenance allowance Rs. 380 to Rs. 1,200 per month depending on course and hostel. Course fee reimbursement. Study tour charges. Book allowance. Thesis typing and printing charges for research scholars.
documents: Income certificate (family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh)
apply_steps: Apply online at scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal)
Register with Aadhaar and select Post Matric Scholarship for SC
Upload caste certificate, income certificate, and mark sheet
Institution verifies enrollment online
Scholarship credited to bank account after verification

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for ST Students
description: Post Matric Scholarship provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe students for post-secondary education. It covers tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other study expenses to enable tribal students to pursue higher education.
ministry: Ministry of Tribal Affairs
benefits: Maintenance allowance and full fee reimbursement for ST students in post-secondary courses. Covers graduation, post-graduation, PhD, professional courses, and vocational training.
apply_steps: Apply at scholarships.gov.in
Select Post Matric Scholarship for ST under Ministry of Tribal Affairs
Fill details and upload ST certificate and income proof
Institution verifies and forwards
Amount credited to bank account

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
description: Post Matric Scholarship for OBC students provides financial assistance to Other Backward Classes students pursuing post-secondary education. It covers maintenance allowance and partial fee reimbursement.
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
benefits: Maintenance allowance and course fee reimbursement for OBC students in post-matric courses. Priority to students in professional and technical courses.
apply_steps: Apply at scholarships.gov.in
Select OBC Post Matric Scholarship scheme
Upload OBC certificate and income proof
Institution verification required
Scholarship disbursed to bank account

description: NMMSS aims to award scholarships to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their dropout at Class 8 and encourage them to continue study at secondary stage. Scholarships are given to students from Class 9 to 12.
ministry: Ministry of Education
benefits: Rs. 12,000 per annum (Rs. 1,000 per month) for students from Class 9 to 12. One lakh fresh scholarships awarded every year. Renewed annually on passing each class.
documents: Class 8 mark sheet with minimum 55% marks
Income certificate (family income below Rs. 1.5 lakh)
apply_steps: Appear in state-level selection test in Class 8 (conducted by state governments)
Selected students apply at scholarships.gov.in
Fill NMMSS application with income and academic details
School verifies enrollment
Rs. 1,000 per month credited to bank account

description: Samagra Shiksha is an integrated scheme for school education extending from pre-school to class 12. It provides free textbooks, uniforms, mid-day meals, and transport allowance to children from disadvantaged sections to improve enrollment and retention.
ministry: Ministry of Education
benefits: Free textbooks, free uniforms (2 sets per year), mid-day meal, transport allowance Rs. 6,000/year for children beyond 1km, free bicycles for girls in Class 9 in many states, residential schools for tribal children.
benefit_amount: Free books, uniforms, meals + ₹6,000 transport allowance
apply_steps: Enroll child in nearest government school
School provides free textbooks and uniforms automatically
Mid-day meal provided daily at school
Transport allowance paid for students beyond 1km distance
No separate application needed — benefits given through school

description: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao scheme aims to address the declining Child Sex Ratio and promote welfare and education of the girl child. It focuses on preventing gender-biased sex selective elimination and ensuring education and participation of girls.
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
benefits: Financial incentives for girl child education, awareness campaigns, improved service delivery for girls. Links with Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana for savings. School enrollment drives and scholarship linkages.
benefit_amount: Incentives through linked schemes like SSY
apply_steps: Contact nearest Anganwadi centre for awareness and services
Open Sukanya Samriddhi account for girl child at post office
Ensure girl child is enrolled in school
Access linked benefits through ICDS, health, and education departments
Report sex-selective practices to district authorities

scheme_name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
description: DDU-GKY funds skill training projects that target rural poor youth between 15 and 35 years. It provides placement-linked skill training with residential facilities, post-placement support, and migration support for rural youth.
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
benefits: Free residential skill training with food and accommodation. Industry-recognized certification. Guaranteed placement with minimum Rs. 6,000 salary. Post-placement support for 2 years. Migration support for outstation placements.
documents: BPL card or MGNREGS job card
apply_steps: Contact nearest DDU-GKY training centre or District Rural Development Agency
Register with Aadhaar and age proof
Choose from available skill courses in retail, hospitality, construction, healthcare etc.
Attend residential training program (free food and stay included)
Placement provided by training partner after certification

description: Startup India aims to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups in India. It provides tax exemptions, easier compliance, access to funds, and mentorship to recognized startups across India.
ministry: Ministry of Commerce and Industry
benefits: 3-year income tax exemption. 80% rebate on patent filing fees. Fast-track exit mechanism. Access to Rs. 10,000 crore Fund of Funds. Free legal support. Credit guarantee through SIDBI.
benefit_amount: Tax exemptions + subsidized patent fees + fund access
apply_steps: Register company or LLP under MCA
Apply for DPIIT recognition at startupindia.gov.in
Submit self-certification of startup criteria (innovation, scalability, 10 years old or less)
Recognition granted within 2 working days
Access benefits like tax exemption, IP support, and funding

description: Stand Up India facilitates bank loans between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 1 crore to at least one SC or ST borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services, or trading sector.
ministry: Ministry of Finance
benefits: Composite loan between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 1 crore covering 75% of project cost. 7-year repayment period with 18-month moratorium. Covers manufacturing, services, and trading enterprises.
benefit_amount: Loan ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore at concessional rate
apply_steps: Apply online at standupmitra.in or visit nearest bank branch
Fill application with project details and personal information
Submit SC/ST certificate and project report
Bank assesses viability and sanctions loan
Loan disbursed in tranches as project progresses

description: PMRPY incentivizes employers to create new employment by paying the full employer EPF contribution for new employees earning up to Rs. 15,000 per month for 3 years. This reduces hiring cost for employers and formalizes employment.
ministry: Ministry of Labour and Employment
benefits: Government pays 12% employer EPF contribution for 3 years for new employees earning up to Rs. 15,000 per month. Makes formal employment cheaper for employers, increasing job creation.
benefit_amount: 12% EPF employer contribution paid by government for 3 years
apply_steps: New employee joins registered EPFO employer
Employer registers on PMRPY portal
Employee's Aadhaar linked to UAN (Universal Account Number)
Government pays employer's 12% EPF share for 3 years automatically
Employee benefits from formal employment and EPF coverage

scheme_name: ESIC Medical and Cash Benefits for Workers
description: Employees State Insurance Corporation provides comprehensive medical care and cash benefits to workers in organized sector. It covers medical treatment, sickness benefit, maternity benefit, disability benefit, and dependent benefit for insured workers.
ministry: Ministry of Labour and Employment
benefits: Free comprehensive medical care for worker and family. Sickness benefit 70% of wages for 91 days per year. Maternity benefit 26 weeks full wages. Disability benefit 90% wages for life. Funeral expenses Rs. 15,000.
apply_steps: Employer registers establishment with ESIC if employing 10 or more workers
Employee automatically enrolled if salary below Rs. 21,000 per month
ESIC card issued to employee
Visit nearest ESIC hospital or dispensary for medical care
Claim cash benefits through employer or online at esic.in

description: EPS provides pension to employees in the organized sector after retirement at age 58. It is part of EPF and covers employees earning up to Rs. 15,000 per month. Pension is based on years of service and last drawn salary.
ministry: Ministry of Labour and Employment
benefits: Monthly pension after retirement at 58. Minimum pension Rs. 1,000 per month. Widow pension, children pension, and orphan pension for dependents. Disability pension for permanent disablement.
apply_steps: Automatically enrolled when employer registers with EPFO
Contribute 12% of basic salary monthly (employer contributes 8.33% to EPS)
Claim pension online at unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in after retirement
Submit Form 10D for pension claim
Pension credited to bank account monthly

description: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendras are model training centres set up in every district of India to provide quality vocational training. They offer aspirational, industry-relevant, and quality skill training with state-of-the-art infrastructure.
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
benefits: Free or subsidized skill training in 100+ job roles across sectors. Modern training infrastructure with labs and equipment. Industry-certified courses. Placement assistance and career guidance.
apply_steps: Find nearest PMKK at skillindiadigital.gov.in
Register online or walk in to training centre
Choose course based on interest and market demand
Complete training (30-300 hours depending on course)
Appear in assessment and receive industry certification

scheme_name: e-SHRAM Portal Registration for Unorganised Workers
description: e-SHRAM is a national database of unorganised workers including construction workers, migrant workers, street vendors, domestic workers, agricultural workers. Registration gives a Universal Account Number and access to social security schemes.
ministry: Ministry of Labour and Employment
benefits: Free registration and Universal Account Number. Accidental insurance of Rs. 2 lakh under PMSBY at no cost for 1 year. Access to social security schemes. Priority in government employment schemes.
documents: Aadhaar card linked to mobile number
apply_steps: Register at eshram.gov.in or nearest CSC
Verify identity with Aadhaar OTP
Fill in occupation, employer details, and bank information
e-SHRAM card downloaded immediately
PMSBY accidental insurance active automatically for 1 year

description: One Stop Centres are established to support women affected by violence in private and public spaces. They provide integrated support services including police facilitation, medical aid, legal aid, psychosocial counseling, and temporary shelter.
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
benefits: Free 24x7 emergency response. Free medical examination and treatment. Free legal aid and counseling. Temporary shelter up to 5 days. Video conferencing for court hearings. Police assistance for FIR.
documents: No documents required for emergency assistance
apply_steps: Call Women Helpline 181 (toll-free, 24x7) in any emergency
Or visit nearest One Stop Centre at District Hospital
All services provided free of cost
No police complaint mandatory for receiving services
Referral to legal aid, shelter, and rehabilitation as needed

description: The Working Women Hostel Scheme provides safe and affordable accommodation to working women in urban, semi-urban, and rural areas where employment opportunities exist. It supports women who migrate for work and need safe housing.
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
benefits: Safe hostel accommodation at subsidized rates. Day care facility for children under 5. Preference to working women from rural areas, single women, and those facing domestic violence.
apply_steps: Contact district Women and Child Development office
Apply for working women hostel accommodation
Submit employment proof and income certificate
Allotment based on availability and income criteria
Monthly rent deducted as per prescribed rates

description: UP state scheme providing accident insurance and income support to farmers and their families. It covers accidental death, permanent disability, and provides free treatment for injuries to farmers of Uttar Pradesh.
ministry: Agriculture Department, Government of Uttar Pradesh
benefits: Accidental death: Rs. 5 lakh to family. Permanent disability: Rs. 5 lakh. Partial disability: Rs. 2.5 lakh. Free treatment up to Rs. 2.5 lakh at empanelled hospitals. Kisan Credit Card holders automatically covered.
benefit_amount: ₹5,00,000 accident cover + free treatment up to ₹2.5 lakh
apply_steps: Farmers with Kisan Credit Card are automatically enrolled
For others: register at nearest UP agriculture office
In case of accident: inform nearest UP Kisaan Bima office within 45 days
Submit FIR, hospital records, and death certificate if applicable
Claim settled within 30 days of document submission

description: Uttar Pradesh government scheme to provide free smartphones and tablets to students studying in government colleges and universities to help them access digital education and online resources.
ministry: Higher Education Department, Government of Uttar Pradesh
benefits: Free smartphone or tablet to students enrolled in government colleges, universities, polytechnics, ITIs, and skill development centres in UP.
apply_steps: Institution registers eligible students on UP Digishakti portal
Students verify details on digishakti.up.gov.in
No separate application required — institution nominates students
Distribution done at college campus
Receive device with pre-loaded educational content

description: Rajasthan state health insurance scheme providing cashless treatment up to Rs. 25 lakh per family per year at government and empanelled private hospitals. It is one of the most comprehensive state health insurance schemes in India.
ministry: Health Department, Government of Rajasthan
benefits: Cashless health coverage up to Rs. 25 lakh per family per year. Covers 1,576 medical packages and procedures. Covers all pre-existing diseases from day one. Free for families registered under NFSA and SECC.
benefit_amount: ₹25,00,000 cashless health coverage per family per year
documents: NFSA or BPL card (for free coverage)
apply_steps: Register on chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in or nearest e-Mitra centre
BPL and NFSA families get free coverage automatically
Other families pay Rs. 850 per year for premium
Get Jan Aadhaar linked to health insurance
Visit any empanelled hospital for cashless treatment

scheme_name: Rajasthan Free Electricity Scheme (100 Units Free)
description: Rajasthan government provides 100 units of free electricity per month to domestic consumers. This benefits poor and middle class families by reducing their electricity bills significantly.
ministry: Energy Department, Government of Rajasthan
benefits: First 100 units of electricity free per month for domestic consumers. Bill generated only if consumption exceeds 100 units. Applicable to single-phase domestic connections.
apply_steps: No separate application required for existing domestic consumers
Benefit applied automatically to electricity bills
New connection: apply at nearest JVVNL, AVVNL, or JDVVNL office
Submit Aadhaar and domicile for new connection
First 100 units deducted automatically from monthly bill

description: MP state housing scheme for urban poor and slum dwellers who are not covered under PMAY. It provides financial assistance for construction of pucca houses to economically weaker sections in urban areas of Madhya Pradesh.
ministry: Urban Development and Housing Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 1.5 lakh to Rs. 2.5 lakh for house construction. Applicable for slum dwellers and urban poor not covered under PMAY. House registered in woman's name.
benefit_amount: ₹1.5 lakh to ₹2.5 lakh for house construction
apply_steps: Apply at Urban Local Body (Nagar Palika or Nagar Panchayat) office
Fill Mukhyamantri Awas Yojana application form
Submit income, Aadhaar, and plot documents
Local body verifies and forwards to state housing board
Assistance released in installments on construction progress

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Jan Kalyan Sambal Yojana (Madhya Pradesh)
description: Sambal Yojana is MP's flagship scheme for unorganised sector workers providing multiple benefits including financial assistance at birth, scholarship, free electricity, and ex-gratia payment on accidental death.
ministry: Labour Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
benefits: Antyesti Sahayata Rs. 5,000 for last rites. Accident death: Rs. 4 lakh to family. Free electricity up to 200 units. Scholarship for children. Birth grant Rs. 16,000 for registered worker's child. Free health check-up.
benefit_amount: Multiple benefits — accident cover ₹4 lakh + free 200 units electricity
apply_steps: Register as Sambal beneficiary at nearest Jan Seva Kendra or nagar panchayat
Submit Aadhaar and BPL certificate
Sambal card issued after verification
Access various benefits using Sambal card
Accident claims submitted at district labour office

description: Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana provides universal health coverage to all residents of Odisha including those not covered by Ayushman Bharat. It covers Rs. 5 lakh for men and Rs. 10 lakh for women per family per year.
ministry: Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Odisha
benefits: Health coverage Rs. 5 lakh per family (Rs. 10 lakh for women) per year. Cashless treatment at all government hospitals. Covers rare diseases up to Rs. 50 lakh under Gopabandhu Jan Arogya Yojana.
apply_steps: Check eligibility at bsky.odisha.gov.in
Visit nearest hospital and show Aadhaar for treatment
Smart health card available at Mo Seva Kendra
Treatment cashless at all government hospitals in Odisha
Private hospital cashless treatment with referral

description: Kanyashree Prakalpa is West Bengal's award-winning conditional cash transfer scheme for girl children to prevent child marriage and keep girls in school. It has been recognised internationally for improving girl education outcomes.
ministry: Women and Child Development and Social Welfare Department, Government of West Bengal
benefits: Annual scholarship of Rs. 1,000 for girls aged 13-18 who are unmarried and enrolled in school. One-time grant of Rs. 25,000 at age 18 if unmarried and enrolled in education or vocational training.
apply_steps: Apply through school (headmaster fills K1 form for annual scholarship)
For one-time grant at 18: apply at BDO or municipality office
Submit school certificate, income proof, and unmarried declaration
District office verifies and approves
Amount credited to girl's bank account

description: Jharkhand state scheme providing financial incentives to unemployed educated youth who have qualified in competitive exams but are waiting for government job posting. It provides monthly financial support during the waiting period.
ministry: Labour Department, Government of Jharkhand
benefits: Rs. 5,000 per month for graduates and Rs. 7,000 per month for post-graduates who have cleared competitive exams but not yet posted. Maximum 2 years support.
apply_steps: Register at rojgar.jharkhand.gov.in
Submit educational and exam qualification certificates
Employment Exchange verifies eligibility
Monthly payment credited to bank account
Stop receiving benefit on getting government job posting

description: Orunodoi is Assam's direct benefit transfer scheme providing financial assistance to economically vulnerable families. The amount is credited directly to the bank account of the woman of the household every month.
ministry: Finance Department, Government of Assam
benefits: Rs. 1,250 per month credited directly to woman's bank account. No conditions attached. Aimed at economically vulnerable families across Assam.
apply_steps: Apply at nearest Block Development Office or Gaon Panchayat
Fill Orunodoi application form
Submit Aadhaar and bank account details of woman
Gaon Burha or Ward Member verifies family details
Monthly transfer starts after verification

description: PMAY-Urban provides affordable housing for urban poor including slum dwellers, economically weaker sections, low income groups, and middle income groups through credit linked subsidy, in-situ slum redevelopment, and affordable housing partnerships.
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
benefits: EWS/LIG: Interest subsidy of 6.5% on home loans up to Rs. 6 lakh. MIG-I: 4% subsidy on loans up to Rs. 9 lakh. MIG-II: 3% subsidy on loans up to Rs. 12 lakh. Slum dwellers get free/subsidized house in-situ.
apply_steps: Apply at nearest Urban Local Body or approved bank/housing finance company
Fill PMAY-U application with income and family details
Select appropriate category EWS LIG MIG
Home loan sanctioned by bank with subsidy applied
Subsidy amount credited upfront reducing loan principal

description: PAHAL is the direct benefit transfer scheme for LPG consumers. The LPG subsidy is directly credited to the bank account of the consumer after they purchase the cylinder at market price. It covers consumers using up to 12 subsidized cylinders per year.
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
benefits: LPG subsidy directly credited to bank account after every cylinder purchase. Up to 12 subsidized cylinders per year. Amount varies with international LPG prices.
benefit_amount: Variable subsidy per cylinder credited to bank account
documents: Aadhaar card linked to LPG connection
apply_steps: Link Aadhaar to LPG consumer account at distributor or online
Link bank account to Aadhaar for subsidy transfer
Book cylinder at market price through normal process
Subsidy amount credited to bank account within 3 days of delivery
Check subsidy status at mylpg.in

scheme_name: National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) - Widow Pension
description: NSAP provides social assistance to poor households in case of old age, death of breadwinner, and maternity. The widow pension component provides monthly pension to widows from BPL households.
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs. 300 for widows aged 40-79 years. Rs. 500 for widows aged 80 years and above. States add their own top-up amount making effective pension Rs. 500 to Rs. 2,000 in most states.
apply_steps: Apply at nearest Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office
Fill NSAP widow pension application form
Submit husband's death certificate and BPL certificate
Gram Panchayat verifies and forwards to BDO
Monthly pension credited to bank account after approval

description: Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections to every rural household by 2024. It is a community-level scheme transforming rural water supply.
ministry: Ministry of Jal Shakti
benefits: Free household tap water connection. Minimum 55 litres per person per day of potable drinking water. Village Water and Sanitation Committee manages local water system.
benefit_amount: Free tap water connection to every rural household
documents: No individual application — village Gram Sabha makes collective request
apply_steps: Gram Sabha passes resolution requesting JJM coverage
District Jal Shakti department surveys village
Water supply scheme designed by state government
Construction done by state PWD or Gram Panchayat
Household tap connection provided free of cost

description: Swachh Bharat Mission provides incentive for construction of individual household latrines to rural households that are below poverty line or have no toilet. It aims to achieve open defecation free status across rural India.
ministry: Ministry of Jal Shakti
benefits: Rs. 12,000 incentive for construction of individual household toilet. Amount released in two installments after construction verified by village level officer.
documents: BPL certificate or household number in SECC
apply_steps: Apply at Gram Panchayat or Block Office
Fill SBM toilet construction application
Gram Panchayat verifies no existing toilet at household
Construct toilet with own funds first
Gram Panchayat official verifies completion and releases Rs. 12,000 to bank account

scheme_name: Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana - NRLM (SHG Bank Linkage)
description: DAY-NRLM mobilizes rural poor women into Self Help Groups and links them to credit and livelihood opportunities. It provides revolving funds, community investment funds, and bank linkage to SHGs for income generation.
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
benefits: Revolving fund of Rs. 15,000 per SHG. Community Investment Fund Rs. 2.5 lakh per SHG. Bank loan linkage at 7% interest (subsidized). Subsidy of Rs. 10,000 to Rs. 15,000 for income generation activities.
benefit_amount: ₹15,000 revolving fund + bank loan at 7% interest
apply_steps: Join nearest Self Help Group in your village or form a new one (10-20 women)
Meet regularly and save together for 3-6 months to establish credibility
Community Resource Person from Block Mission Management Unit will help
Apply for Revolving Fund after 3 months of savings
Bank loan linkage provided after 6 months of regular SHG activity

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA)
description: PMGDISHA aims to make 6 crore rural households digitally literate. It teaches basic digital skills like operating smartphones, using internet, accessing government services online, and making digital payments to rural citizens.
ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
benefits: Free 20-hour digital literacy training. Covers smartphone use, internet browsing, email, government apps, digital payments (UPI, BHIM). Certificate recognized for employment. Priority to non-IT literate households.
apply_steps: Register at pmgdisha.in or visit nearest Common Service Centre
Check if your household is eligible (one non-literate member per household)
Attend 20 hours of digital literacy classes at local training centre
Appear in online assessment exam
Digital literacy certificate issued free of cost

scheme_name: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
description: CGTMSE provides credit guarantee to banks and NBFCs for loans given to micro and small enterprises without collateral. It enables first-generation entrepreneurs without property or assets to get business loans.
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
benefits: Collateral-free business loans up to Rs. 5 crore guaranteed by government. Banks can lend without asking for property or guarantor. Women entrepreneurs get higher guarantee coverage.
benefit_amount: Guarantee on collateral-free loans up to ₹5 crore
apply_steps: Apply for business loan at any CGTMSE member bank or NBFC
Bank evaluates business viability without collateral requirement
Bank obtains CGTMSE guarantee cover automatically
Loan sanctioned based on business merit alone
No need to visit CGTMSE — bank handles guarantee process

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Jan Aushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP)
description: PMBJP provides quality generic medicines at affordable prices through dedicated stores called Jan Aushadhi Kendras. Generic medicines are 50% to 90% cheaper than branded medicines with same quality and efficacy.
ministry: Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers
benefits: Medicines 50-90% cheaper than branded versions. Over 1,900 medicines and 300 surgical items available. Jan Aushadhi Kendras available at railway stations, hospitals, and towns across India.
apply_steps: Find nearest Jan Aushadhi Kendra at janaushadhi.gov.in
Bring doctor's prescription to the store
Generic equivalent provided at much lower cost
Pharmacist explains equivalent generic medicines
No registration required — walk-in purchase

description: NFBS provides lump sum financial assistance to bereaved families below poverty line when the primary breadwinner dies due to any reason. The assistance helps the family cope with the sudden loss of income.
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
benefits: One-time lump sum assistance of Rs. 20,000 to the bereaved family on death of primary breadwinner aged 18-59 years from BPL household.
benefit_amount: ₹20,000 one-time lump sum on death of breadwinner
apply_steps: Apply at nearest Gram Panchayat or Block Office within 90 days of death
Fill NFBS application form
Submit death certificate, BPL certificate, and bank details
Block Development Officer verifies and approves
Rs. 20,000 transferred to bank account within 30 days

description: Karunya Health Scheme provides financial assistance to BPL families in Kerala for treatment of serious illnesses like cancer, kidney disease, and heart disease at government and empanelled private hospitals.
ministry: Health Department, Government of Kerala
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs. 2 lakh per year for serious illnesses. Covers cancer, kidney failure, heart surgery, and other critical conditions. Applicable at government medical colleges and empanelled hospitals.
benefit_amount: Up to ₹2,00,000 per year for critical illness
apply_steps: Apply at District Medical Office or government hospital in Kerala
Submit medical diagnosis and income certificate
District Medical Officer approves based on condition severity and BPL status
Treatment at empanelled hospital with Karunya approval
Bills reimbursed directly to hospital or patient

description: Karnataka state scheme providing direct income support to small and marginal farmers. It supplements central PM-KISAN with additional state-level financial support to improve farmer incomes in Karnataka.
ministry: Agriculture Department, Government of Karnataka
benefits: Rs. 2,000 per year additional income support to farmers. Complements PM-KISAN giving Karnataka farmers a total of Rs. 8,000 per year. Direct bank transfer.
apply_steps: Register at nearest Raitha Samparka Kendra
Submit land records and Aadhaar details
Automatic enrollment if already registered for PM-KISAN
Amount credited to bank account directly
Check status at raitamitra.kar.nic.in

scheme_name: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Breakfast Scheme
description: Tamil Nadu government provides free nutritious breakfast to students studying in government schools from Class 1 to Class 5. It aims to improve school attendance and reduce malnutrition among school children.
ministry: School Education Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
benefits: Free nutritious breakfast every school day for Class 1-5 students in government schools. Menu includes idli, pongal, upma, bread and egg, khichdi. Improves attendance and reduces hunger.
documents: School enrollment in Tamil Nadu government school
apply_steps: No separate application needed
Enroll child in Tamil Nadu government school
Breakfast provided automatically every school day
Nutritious menu prepared by school cook
Parents need not provide morning meal on school days

description: MA Yojana is Gujarat's flagship health insurance scheme providing cashless treatment for serious illnesses to BPL families. It covers tertiary care treatment including cardiac surgery, cancer treatment, kidney transplant, and neurosurgery.
ministry: Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat
benefits: Cashless treatment up to Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for serious illnesses. Covers 990+ procedures including cardiac surgery, cancer, kidney disease, neurosurgery. Free treatment at 1,300+ empanelled hospitals.
benefit_amount: ₹5,00,000 cashless treatment per family per year
apply_steps: Apply for MA Yojana card at nearest Community Health Centre or District Hospital
Submit Aadhaar and BPL/ration card
MA card issued free within 7 days
For treatment: visit any empanelled hospital with MA card
Treatment completely cashless — no payment required

description: YSR Pension Kanuka is Andhra Pradesh's comprehensive pension scheme providing monthly pension to senior citizens, widows, weavers, toddy tappers, fishermen, and persons with disabilities under a unified platform.
ministry: Social Welfare Department, Government of Andhra Pradesh
benefits: Rs. 2,750 per month for senior citizens (60+), widows, disabled, and traditional workers. Pension delivered at home by village secretariat staff on 1st of every month.
documents: Age proof or death certificate of husband for widow
apply_steps: Apply at nearest Village/Ward Secretariat in Andhra Pradesh
Fill YSR Pension application with category details
Village Secretariat staff verifies at home visit
Approval by District Social Welfare Officer
Pension credited and delivered home on 1st of month

description: Rythu Bandhu is Telangana's investment support scheme for farmers providing Rs. 10,000 per acre per year for farm investment. It is one of India's most generous state-level farmer income support programs.
ministry: Agriculture Department, Government of Telangana
benefits: Rs. 10,000 per acre per year (Rs. 5,000 per season) directly to farmer's bank account. Covers all land-owning farmers in Telangana regardless of crop grown.
apply_steps: Automatic enrollment based on land records in Telangana
Verify pattadar passbook details at nearest Agriculture Office if needed
Ensure Aadhaar linked to bank account
Amount credited to bank twice a year before Kharif and Rabi seasons
Check status at rythubandhu.telangana.gov.in

scheme_name: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana (Maharashtra)
description: Maharashtra state health insurance scheme providing cashless hospitalization to yellow and orange ration card holders. It covers surgeries and therapies at government and empanelled private hospitals across Maharashtra.
ministry: Public Health Department, Government of Maharashtra
benefits: Cashless treatment up to Rs. 5 lakh per family per year (Rs. 1.5 lakh for Ayushman Bharat + additional state top-up). Covers 971 medical and surgical procedures at empanelled hospitals.
benefit_amount: ₹5,00,000 cashless treatment per family per year
apply_steps: Obtain MJPJAY health card at nearest government hospital or CSC
Submit ration card and Aadhaar
Card issued free within 15 minutes at hospital
For treatment: visit any empanelled hospital with MJPJAY card
All cashless — no payment at hospital

description: Haryana government provides multiple benefits to farmers including bonus on MSP crops, accident insurance, and pension for aged farmers. It supplements central schemes with state-level support for Haryana farmers.
ministry: Agriculture Department, Government of Haryana
benefits: MSP bonus for selling crops at Haryana mandis. Accident insurance Rs. 5 lakh for farmers. Crop diversification incentive Rs. 7,000 per acre for switching from paddy to other crops.
benefit_amount: Bonus on MSP + ₹5 lakh accident cover
apply_steps: Sell crop at nearest Haryana mandi to get J-Form
MSP bonus credited automatically to bank account
For accident insurance: register at nearest Agriculture Office
Crop diversification incentive: apply at block agriculture office with crop change proof
Payments through DBT to bank account

scheme_name: National Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities
description: Scholarship scheme providing financial assistance to students with benchmark disabilities for pursuing higher education. It covers pre-matric, post-matric, and top class education scholarships for disabled students.
ministry: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities
benefits: Pre-matric: Rs. 700 to Rs. 1,000 per month. Post-matric: Rs. 2,000 to Rs. 3,500 per month plus fee reimbursement. Top class: full fee, living expenses, and book grant for students in top institutions.
documents: Disability certificate (minimum 40% disability from competent authority)
apply_steps: Apply at scholarships.gov.in under DEPwD scholarship
Select appropriate level (pre-matric/post-matric/top class)
Upload disability certificate and income proof
Institution verifies disability and enrollment
Scholarship credited quarterly to bank account

scheme_name: ADIP Scheme (Assistance to Disabled Persons for Aids and Appliances)
description: ADIP scheme provides modern and durable assistive devices to persons with disabilities to restore their physical and economic capabilities. It provides devices free or at subsidized cost to disabled persons below poverty line.
ministry: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities
benefits: Free assistive devices for income below Rs. 15,000 per month. 50% subsidy for income Rs. 15,000 to Rs. 20,000. Devices include wheelchairs, hearing aids, artificial limbs, white canes, tricycles, braille kits, and cochlear implants.
apply_steps: Contact nearest ALIMCO office or district disability rehabilitation centre
Medical assessment to determine required device
Submit disability certificate and income certificate
Device provided free or at subsidy based on income
Fitting and training provided for complex devices like limbs
